description.content: OIDC authorization endpoints for initiating authentication flows and exchanging authorization codes for tokens.

Initiates an OpenID Connect authorization flow. The client redirects the user to this endpoint with the required parameters. On successful authentication, the OpenID Provider redirects back to the client's redirect_uri with an authorization code (Authorization Code Flow) or tokens (Implicit Flow).

query.description: (Required) Specifies the authorization flow. Use 'code' for Authorization Code Flow, 'id_token' for Implicit Flow.
(Required) The client identifier registered with the OpenID Provider
(Required) URI to redirect to after authentication. Must match a pre-registered redirect URI for the client.
(Required) Space-separated list of scopes. Must include 'openid'. Additional scopes: profile, email, address, phone, offline_access.
(Required) Opaque value used to maintain state between the request and callback. Used to prevent CSRF attacks.
String value used to associate a client session with an ID token and mitigate replay attacks.
PKCE code challenge derived from the code_verifier. Required for public clients using PKCE.
Method used to derive the code_challenge from code_verifier
Mechanism used to return authorization response parameters
Prompts for user interaction during authentication
Hint to the authorization server about the login identifier

response.name: Redirect to redirect_uri with authorization code or tokens

header.description: Redirect URL with code and state parameters (Authorization Code Flow) or tokens in fragment (Implicit Flow)

description.content: Token endpoint operations for exchanging authorization codes and refresh tokens for access tokens and ID tokens.

Exchanges an authorization code for an access token, ID token, and optionally a refresh token. Also used to refresh expired access tokens using a refresh token. Supports client_secret_basic and client_secret_post authentication methods.

urlencoded.description: (Required) The grant type for the token request
Authorization code received from the authorization endpoint. Required for authorization_code grant.
Must match the redirect_uri used in the authorization request. Required for authorization_code grant.
Client identifier. Required for client_secret_post authentication.
Client secret. Required for client_secret_post authentication.
PKCE code verifier. Required when code_challenge was used in the authorization request.
Refresh token for obtaining new access tokens. Required for refresh_token grant.
Optional scope for refresh_token grant. Must be a subset of originally granted scopes.

Revokes an access token or refresh token. The token is immediately invalidated and can no longer be used for API access or token refresh.

urlencoded.description: (Required) The access token or refresh token to revoke
Optional hint about the token type being revoked

response.name: Token successfully revoked (or was already invalid)

Optional hint about the token type being revoked

UserInfo endpoint for retrieving authenticated user profile claims.
Returns claims about the authenticated user. The access token is passed as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. The claims returned depend on the scopes granted during authorization.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Returns claims about the authenticated user via POST request. The access token is passed as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
OpenID Provider Discovery endpoint for retrieving provider configuration metadata.

Returns the OpenID Provider's configuration metadata as defined in RFC 8414 (OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server Metadata) and the OIDC Discovery specification. Clients use this endpoint to discover all required endpoints, supported features, and public keys.
JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) endpoint for retrieving public keys used to verify ID token signatures.

Returns the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) containing the public keys used by the OpenID Provider to sign ID tokens and other JWTs. Clients use these keys to verify the signature of ID tokens received from the token endpoint.

The OpenID Connect (OIDC) API is a lightweight identity layer built on top of OAuth 2.0 that enables applications to verify user identity and obtain basic profile information. OIDC defines standard endpoints including the Authorization Endpoint, Token Endpoint, UserInfo Endpoint, and JWKS URI. It supports Authorization Code Flow, Implicit Flow, Hybrid Flow, and PKCE extensions for public clients. OIDC is widely implemented by identity providers including Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, Google, Auth0, and Keycloak.
